FlowCore Guide: iLabs Billing 

Go to “Schedule Equipment” tab under the FlowCore page 

Graphical user interface, application, website

Description automatically generated 

Click “Review Usage” for the desired instrument 

 

Make sure date range is appropriate for what you are trying to bill for (should automatically default to 30 before current date) 

Graphical user interface, text, application

Description automatically generated 

Make sure that usage time and payment information is correct for the given time slot 

 

Determining if an appointment needs to be fixed: 

Payment info is not correct 

If payment is fine:  

Go to step 5 

If payment method is close to expiration:  

Make sure expiration date is after the current appointment 

Go to step 5 

If payment info is incorrect or missing:  

See if the user has a Project ID associated with their account. Ask user to fill out Project information on iLabs 

If no account information found, contact Tom McHugh 

Time should be adjusted 

Adjust the time for the appointment via the calendar 

Sometimes a warning on the payment method will show up if the user has time scheduled past the “open” times of the instrument, to fix: 

Expand the “Pricing Details” section 

Graphical user interface, text, application, email

Description automatically generated 

Fill in the missing time segments (you may need to check the override box) 

Text

Description automatically generated 

Change the missing billing info for the time slot 

Graphical user interface, text, application, chat or text message

Description automatically generated 

 Click “Save Reservation” 

Billing Special Cases: 

Aria II gets a 1-time set up fee charged for each appointment 

No Charge (click the no charge checkbox): 

Aurora for data analysis only 

There is a Journal Entry for the user/user’s lab 

As determined by staff 

If billing is correct for a given appointment, click the check mark 

 

Once all slots are reviewed, click “confirm all checked” 

Graphical user interface, text, application, chat or text message

Description automatically generated 

Repeat for all instruments in the core.
